banner.title: Ready to ship with a cleaner foundation?
banner.subtitle: Spin up your first Mavrynt module in under ten minutes.

footer.tagline: The modular monolith platform for modern product teams.

home.title: Ship faster with a modular monolith you can trust.
home.subtitle: Mavrynt gives your team a clean foundation to build, deploy, and scale — without drowning in microservices.

logos.title: Built with care by teams at

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Everything your team needs to build durable product software — without ceremony.

modular.title: Modular by default
modular.desc: Bounded modules keep ownership crisp and refactors safe. No premature distributed systems.

typed.title: Type-safe, end to end
typed.desc: Strict TypeScript in the frontend, nullable-aware C# in the backend, contract-first APIs in between.

observable.title: Observable out of the box
observable.desc: Structured logs, traces, and health checks ship preconfigured — not as an afterthought.

secure.title: Secure by design
secure.desc: Sensible defaults for auth, secrets, and CSP. Security headers and threat models baked in.

scalable.title: Scales with you
scalable.desc: Start as a single deployable, split a module into its own service the day you need to.

extensible.title: Extensible through ports
extensible.desc: Ports and adapters everywhere: swap analytics, email, payments, or search without rewriting callers.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Three moves from zero to first value.

1.title: Start clean
1.desc: Clone the template, pick your modules, and boot locally in minutes.

2.title: Build features
2.desc: Add capabilities inside bounded modules. The platform keeps contracts and observability consistent.

3.title: Scale with confidence
3.desc: Ship to production with tracing, feature flags, and zero-downtime deploys already wired up.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Simple plans that grow with your product. No per-seat surprises.

starter.name: Starter
starter.desc: For solo developers exploring the platform.

team.name: Team
team.desc: For growing teams shipping real product.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.desc: For regulated orgs that need guarantees.

testimonials.title: Teams shipping with Mavrynt
testimonials.subtitle: Stories from engineering leaders who stopped drowning in services and started shipping features.

1.quote: We collapsed four half-built services back into a Mavrynt monolith and halved our incident rate in a quarter.
1.author: Ana Kowalski

2.quote: The module boundaries are real. New engineers find their way in a day, not a week.
2.author: Marek Novák

3.quote: Observability was the thing we always pushed to next quarter. Mavrynt shipped it on day one.
3.author: Lena Sørensen

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Can't find what you're looking for? Reach out — we answer every message.

1.a: The core template and module SDK are open source under MIT. Paid tiers add tooling, support, and enterprise features.

2.q: Why a modular monolith and not microservices?
2.a: Monoliths with hard module boundaries are simpler to operate for most teams. When a module genuinely needs to scale independently, Mavrynt makes extraction safe and boring.

3.a: .NET 10 on the backend, React 19 + Vite on the frontend, Postgres by default, OpenTelemetry for observability. Adapters let you swap any of these.

4.a: Yes — all tiers support self-hosting. Enterprise adds air-gapped installs and signed artifacts.

5.a: Semantic versioning on the SDK, explicit migration guides on every minor, and automated codemods for the common cases.

6.a: Yes. Tenancy is a first-class concern — row-level isolation, per-tenant config, and metrics tagging are built in.
